hard.next_step: Confidence 25% 2nd Jul email outreach to Pracha &Supakit on status 4th May- sent follow upi email to drive ROI workshop 22nd Apr- Zycus has been advised we are shortlisted for RFP mid/late May Prospect met us at the BKK Roundtable in November 2025- SAP Renewal in the year 2026, exploring solutions in the market. Post which a conf call is scheduled for 18th February 2026
hard.ais_why: Buyer is interested in AI-driven intake (Merlin) but immediately paired it with configuration-complexity concern on the 18 Feb 2026 discovery call; his framing is reactive (escape Ariba admin pain) not visionary. Product scope is AI-tilted (eProcurement;iSaaS;Merlin Intake;ANA). Kept at 6 (Moderate) because AI interest came with a backend-difficulty doubt, not unbridled enthusiasm.

north_star_verdict.headline: Dark for ~138 days after a single 18 Feb discovery call: the RFP promised 'by June' never arrived, four rep touches drew no buyer reply, and the deal is single-threaded through Pracha (Evaluator) while Supakit (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker) has never engaged. The real threat is renew-Ariba-by-default, not a rival. 86 days to a 30 Sep close is not credible without immediate buyer re-engagement.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-02-09 (147 days old). Qualified 2026-02-27; moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation 2026-04-22 (rep-driven, 75 days in stage). Last verified two-way buyer engagement: 2026-02-18 discovery call (138 days ago). Rep-only touches since: 26 Mar (could-not-connect x2), 22 Apr call note 'RFP expected by June', 04 May ROI-workshop email, 02 Jul status email (awaiting reply). RFP promised mid/late May never issued. Close 2026-09-30 is 86 days out; a full-suite SAP replacement would need shortlist/selection by end-July, commercials in August, contracting in September against a buyer that has not moved past one discovery call. Forward slip: close was already pulled in from 2027-07-31 to 2026-09-30 on 22 Apr, an aspirational date.
evidence: Last two-way buyer engagement 2026-02-18 discovery call, 138 days ago; nothing since (source: Avoma call 2026-02-18, SF LastActivityDate)
RFP promised mid/late May 2026 never issued (source: Next_Step, Task 2026-04-22 'RFP expected by June')
Four rep touches drew no buyer reply: 26 Mar could-not-connect x2, 04 May ROI email, 02 Jul status email (source: Tasks 2026-03-26 to 2026-07-02)
Single-threaded: only Pracha (Evaluator) ever engaged; Supakit (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) has never engaged (source: Contact Roles, Avoma 2026-02-18)
SAP Ariba is the incumbent, year 4 of a 5-year contract with a 2026 SAP renewal; renew-by-default inertia is the dominant threat (source: Avoma 2026-02-18, Next_Step)
No quantified business case: qualitative Ariba pain only, no spend/cycle-time/savings numbers (source: Avoma 2026-02-18)

metrics.narrative: Pracha Khusakul articulated real qualitative pain on the 18 Feb 2026 discovery call but never quantified it: decentralized procurement across Thailand, Sri Lanka and Vietnam runs on one on-prem SAP S/4HANA with a web of siloed systems (Ariba SLP/Sourcing/Network, blockchain e-bank-guarantee, DocuSign, a SharePoint contract tool, an in-house vendor 'window' portal, and RPA bots). He said building a single catalog forces 'my team have to connect with the IT team, the sub IT of the supplier, and the Ariba team, 4 party to working together.' No spend-under-management, sourcing cycle time, headcount, or savings target was ever stated, and the 04 May ROI-workshop email went unanswered. Quantifying the switching-cost case versus the Ariba renewal is the move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No engaged economic buyer. Budget authority for a $200k full-suite SAP-integration purchase sits at or above the Thailand Head of Procurement: Pracha described himself as running 'operation procurement... the purchasing part and the system part' and said 'I have my board that head of procurement in Thailand, and we have a manager,' placing power above him. Supakit Kitsaran carries the Salesforce title Head of Procurement and the Decision Maker contact role, so he is the named budget owner, but he has never engaged across four rep attempts (26 Mar to 02 Jul). Carl also probed 'do you have a CPO?' and got no name. The move is to get Supakit into a call and confirm who signs a spend of this size.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Supakit Kitsaran, Head of Procurement, Decision Maker)

decision_criteria.narrative: From the 18 Feb call the buyer's evaluation criteria are: AI-driven intake usability (Pracha: interest in 'a guided, AI-driven user experience enforcing policies'), ease of backend configuration (his explicit doubt: 'I'm not sure behind the system how difficult to configuration this one'), SAP S/4HANA integration credibility (Carl countered with '70% of our customers run SAP'), category workflow flexibility across their three category teams (direct materials, services, MRO/capex), and a phased versus big-bang migration path. These are confirmed from the call but not yet formalized into an RFP scorecard; the promised RFP that would document them never arrived.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-02-18 (Pracha Khusakul, Carl Kimball)

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown because it never ran past discovery. Pracha closed the 18 Feb call saying 'I think we should have a next meeting, and I will invite more people like our category sourcing team,' and both an IT deep-dive and a category-team demo were floated, but none was scheduled and the buyer has been silent for 138 days. The only structural forcing function is contractual: the buyer is in year 4 of a 5-year Ariba contract with a 2026 SAP renewal, so a switch-or-renew decision is due within about a year. The move is to get the RFP timeline and renewal date confirmed in writing to reconstruct the process.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced: no MSA/SOW, legal, procurement channel, or InfoSec discussion has occurred, consistent with a deal still at a single discovery call. The one live forcing function is the Ariba 5-year contract (currently year 4) and the 2026 SAP renewal, which sets a natural switch-or-renew window. Visibility would come from getting the buyer to name the renewal date, the RFP issuance date, and their procurement/approval path in writing.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated (qualitatively) and owned by the operations procurement and system-admin team. Pracha and his system admin called administering Ariba 'really difficult... it might be very patient to support,' described a fragmented landscape of siloed systems that must be manually linked, a SharePoint-based contract tool worked 'quite manually,' and RPA bots that in parts are 'not working properly.' Building a catalog needs a four-party effort (their team, supplier IT, Ariba, and the Alibaba/Ariba team). Carl also surfaced a cost pain: 'Ariba is starting to become expensive too... CFOs don't see a good ROI with Ariba.' The pain is real and current; it is simply not yet quantified.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-02-18 (Pracha Khusakul, system admin, Carl Kimball)

champion.narrative: No champion. Pracha Khusakul (Sourcing manager, Evaluator) was engaged and collaborative on the 18 Feb call, articulated the pain, liked the Merlin Teams-embedded intake demo, and floated inviting his category team, but he is an operational evaluator without authority, and he has been silent for 138 days, did not deliver the promised RFP, and did not answer the 04 May or 02 Jul emails. That decays him from a potential coach to a cold contact, not a champion. Supakit Kitsaran, the named Decision Maker, has never engaged. What is missing is an internal advocate with access to the budget owner who will drive the evaluation; securing one ranks above any commercial step.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the entrenched incumbent (buyer runs Ariba SLP, Ariba Sourcing and Ariba Network on top of SAP S/4HANA) and the buyer is genuinely pained by it, but there is no active rival bake-off, so the dominant competitive threat is renew-Ariba-by-default and do-nothing inertia, amplified by the 138-day silence and the un-issued RFP. Zycus's wedge is Merlin Teams-embedded intake, agentic ANA negotiation, native SAP integration ('70% of our customers run SAP'), and a modular phased replacement rather than a big-bang rip-out. We win by proving a low-risk phased SAP migration and quantifying Ariba renewal cost versus Zycus savings before the renewal decision is made by default.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-02-18 (Carl Kimball, Pracha Khusakul)

deal_movement.summary: One buyer touch (18 Feb discovery call), then rep-only activity. Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 22 Apr and close pulled in from 2027-07-31 to 2026-09-30 the same day, both rep-driven with no buyer action behind them. Buyer silent 138 days; last touch is a 02 Jul rep email awaiting reply.

items.change: Discovery/overview call held with Pracha Khusakul and his system-admin team; Ariba pain surfaced, Merlin intake demoed
Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation (rep-driven)
Close date pulled in from 2027-07-31 to 2026-09-30 (rep-driven)
ROI-workshop follow-up email sent, no buyer reply
Status-check email sent to Pracha and Supakit, awaiting reply

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: it is the incumbent (Ariba SLP/Sourcing/Network on SAP S/4HANA), the buyer is in year 4 of a 5-year contract with a 2026 SAP renewal, and although Pracha called it 'really difficult' to administer on 18 Feb, incumbent inertia plus IT familiarity plus embedded SAP integration make a renew-by-default the most likely outcome given the 138-day silence and the RFP that never issued. No competitive bake-off is running and no other vendor has been named as an SCCC evaluation rival (Carl's passing mention of Coupa/Ivalua was generic market commentary, not this buyer's shortlist). The practical second threat is do-nothing/status-quo: the buyer keeps managing a fragmented Ariba landscape and simply renews.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: It's really difficult. I can say that it's really difficult. It might be very patient to support.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-02-18 (Pracha Khusakul, on administering Ariba)
competitors.how_we_win: Prove a low-risk phased SAP S/4HANA migration and quantify the Ariba renewal cost versus Zycus savings before the year-4 renewal decision is made by default; lead with Merlin Teams-embedded intake as the capability Ariba cannot match.

competitors.name: Do nothing / renew Ariba by default
competitors.quote: We are under the 5 year contract, and this is the fourth year. So this year we have to consider to continue or switching the procurement portal.
competitors.how_we_win: Convert the switch-or-renew window into a compelling event: get the renewal date and RFP timeline in writing, and put a quantified savings case in front of the Head of Procurement so inaction has a visible cost.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Interest is centered on AI-driven intake usability, where Zycus fits well, but the buyer's headline doubt is backend configuration difficulty and unproven SAP integration, which are still exposed.

items.criterion: Ease of backend configuration and administration

We have nearly 70% of our customers run SAP. So we have a connector, and we have many, many experience working with SAP, even the old ECC and s4 HANA.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-02-18 (Carl Kimball, claim not yet proven to their IT)

items.criterion: Phased, modular replacement rather than big-bang rip-out
items.quote: If you wanna do a phased model where you just start to pull out pieces and replace them, you need to think about the future.
I have my board that head of procurement in Thailand, and we have a manager.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is dark and single-threaded at an early stage; the win plan is to break the silence with a value hook, multi-thread to power and IT, and anchor the compelling event.
flags: Break the 138-day silence: have Carl reach Pracha and Supakit with a specific Ariba renewal-cost hook, not another status chase.
Multi-thread beyond Pracha: Supakit (Decision Maker) and the group IT lead have never engaged, so no power sponsorship or SAP integration validation exists.
Anchor the compelling event: the year-4-of-5 Ariba contract and 2026 SAP renewal are the only forcing function, so pin the renewal date and RFP timeline in writing.

items.name: Supakit Kitsaran
items.title: Head of Procurement
items.risk: Named Decision Maker and the most plausible budget owner for a $200k purchase, but has never engaged across four rep attempts (26 Mar to 02 Jul). No power sponsorship exists until he is in a conversation; he may lack urgency or defer to a renewal.

items.risk: Sole engaged contact and the only source of pain and interest, but an operational evaluator without authority and now silent 138 days; did not deliver the promised RFP or answer the 04 May and 02 Jul emails.

Pracha (Sourcing manager, Evaluator) engaged well on 18 Feb but is an operational evaluator without authority and has gone cold for 138 days, delivering no RFP and no reply to the 04 May or 02 Jul emails. Supakit (Decision Maker) has never engaged, so there is no internal advocate driving the evaluation or providing access to the budget owner.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Carl led with Merlin Teams-embedded intake and agentic ANA negotiation, and the intake demo genuinely interested Pracha, but the AI story surfaced a backend configuration-complexity doubt that was never resolved and the buyer's framing stayed reactive (escape Ariba admin pain) rather than autonomous-procurement visionary. The AI narrative opened the door but was left half-landed.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 6 (Moderate). Calls corroborate: real interest in Merlin AI intake ('a guided, AI-driven user experience') but paired immediately with a configuration-difficulty concern, so appetite is curious not hungry. The field rating is accurate, not under- or over-stated.

Buyer is wary of a big-bang replacement of an embedded Ariba/SAP landscape and flagged backend configuration difficulty on 18 Feb.
SAP S/4HANA on-prem integration is unproven to the buyer's IT, who have not engaged.

critical_signals.text: SAP Ariba is the entrenched incumbent in year 4 of 5 and the buyer is pained by it, but a renewal-by-default is the real threat, not an active rival.
Supakit Kitsaran, the Head of Procurement and named decision maker, has never engaged despite four rep attempts since March.
The only real conversation was the 18 Feb discovery overview, where Pracha liked the Merlin intake demo but flagged backend configuration complexity, and it has gone silent since.
No price, ROI, or savings case has been discussed, so a $200k full-suite switch from Ariba has no business case behind it.

scope_change.detail: Scope has held at eProcurement, iSaaS, Merlin Intake and ANA since creation; the only field move was the close date being pulled in from 2027-07-31 to 2026-09-30 on 22 Apr, not a product or amount reduction.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Dark for ~138 days after a single 18 Feb discovery call: the promised RFP never came, four rep touches drew no reply, and with no champion, no engaged budget owner, and an embedded Ariba/SAP incumbent, this is a no-decision / renew-Ariba risk more than a live opportunity.

win_position.text: We opened well, with Pracha calling Ariba 'really difficult' and liking the Merlin Teams-embedded intake demo on 18 Feb, but the deal has since gone silent with no champion, no engaged budget owner, and an entrenched SAP Ariba incumbent (Avoma 2026-02-18).
Pracha's own doubt, 'I'm not sure behind the system how difficult to configuration this one,' is an unresolved fit objection that plays straight into an Ariba renewal (Avoma 2026-02-18).
The buyer is in year 4 of a 5-year Ariba contract on SAP S/4HANA, so renew-by-default inertia, not a competing vendor, is the single thing most likely to lose this (Avoma 2026-02-18).

deal_momentum.text: No buyer response in ~138 days since the 18 Feb call, the RFP promised 'by June' never arrived, and Supakit has never engaged across four attempts (Tasks 2026-04-22, 2026-07-02).
Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 22 Apr and the close date was pulled in the same day, but both were rep actions with no buyer action behind them (field history 2026-04-22).

customer_commitment.text: Pracha floated inviting his category sourcing leads and an IT deep-dive but never scheduled either, and asked only for a deck 'to share with my team' (Avoma 2026-02-18).
Engagement never left one generic overview call: no pricing, security, legal, or procurement conversation has started (Avoma 2026-02-18).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded through Pracha (Evaluator), now silent, while Supakit (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker) has never engaged across four touches (Contact Roles; Tasks 2026-03-26 to 2026-07-02).
A $200k full-suite replacement of an embedded Ariba/SAP S/4HANA stack has no IT lead engaged and no quantified savings case (Avoma 2026-02-18).

deal_momentum.evidence: 138 days of buyer silence, no RFP, stage advanced only by the rep on 22 Apr

customer_commitment.evidence: engagement never left one generic overview call; no pricing/security/legal started; only a deck was requested

deal_risk.evidence: single-threaded through a now-silent evaluator; Decision Maker Supakit never engaged; embedded Ariba/SAP incumbency

customer_preference.evidence: Interested in Merlin AI intake but paired it with a backend-config doubt, requested only a deck for his team, and then went silent for 138 days: 'I heard that already but I'm not sure behind the system how difficult to configuration this one' (Pracha, 2026-02-18).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative Ariba pain only (admin 'really difficult', siloed systems, four-party catalog builds); no spend, cycle-time, or savings figure quantified, and the ROI-workshop offer (04 May) went unanswered.
